sweetness.label: dry leaning
sweetness.description: Bone-dry leaning. Spirit-forward palate. Rejects overly sweet builds; dessert-adjacent only when the framing earns it (e.g., milk punches, after-dinner sippers).

acid.label: situational
acid.description: Comfortable in both lanes — strongly enjoys stirred no-citrus drinks (Hallucination, Manhattan-family) AND citrus-forward shaken drinks (Crossover, Margarita-family). Picks based on occasion.

strength.label: spirit-forward
strength.description: Spirit-forward. Self-described preference for 'spirit-forward classic cocktail traditions.' Most originals are 1.5+ oz of base spirit with small-pour modifiers.

complexity.label: layered and brooding
complexity.description: Loves bitters stacking, split modifiers, and structural complexity. Owns 10 bitters SKUs and uses them deliberately. Originals consistently feature 2–3 bitters working in conversation.

season.label: adapts with season
season.description: Has explicitly built warm-weather originals (Wild Sanibel) and contemplative cool-weather originals (The Hallucination). Active about seasonal context.

risk.label: surprise me
risk.description: Has six originals on file, actively iterates on them, asks for 'new and different than just a margarita with a mezcal substitute.' Wants creative pushback, not safe defaults.

smoke.rationale: Inferred from inventory (Turkish Tobacco bitters, Cherry Maple Smoked bitters, Montelobos Espadin mezcal) and originals (Crossover, Smokey the Pear, Dave's Folly all incorporate smoke meaningfully).

funk.rationale: Inferred from inventory (both white and dark Jamaican rum stocked — ester-forward category) and Smokey the Pear v2 explicitly preferring white Jamaican rum as sub-base for ester complementarity with pear.

savory_saline.rationale: No strong evidence of preference or aversion in documented originals or inventory. Worth exploring.

background.drinking_frequency: Regular home cocktail-maker; treats mixology as a craft and ongoing creative project
background.profession: Vice President of Decision Sciences. Leads an 8-9 person team of PhDs specializing in predictive modeling (supervised and unsupervised learning). Physics PhD. Strong applied background in financial modeling for investment predictions.
background.typical_context: Solo evenings (contemplative, often spirit-forward); entertaining friends and family; occasional themed drinks for specific guests (e.g., habanero-loving Dave).
background.household_context: Wife is Taiwanese — household has access to many Asian ingredients (yuzu, shiso, sake, mirin, matcha, Asian pear, dried plums, oolong, jasmine tea, black sesame potentially available; specifics still TBD). When designing drinks, consider whether these ingredients would unlock distinctive builds and ask if relevant.
background.vocabulary_preference: Free use of physics, data science, predictive modeling, and financial/investment terminology is welcome and encouraged.
background.other_notes: Maintains an organized, well-stocked bar with particular depth in whisk(e)y (5 regional styles), bitters (10 SKUs), and an interesting fortified wine and liqueur collection. Keeps an active shopping list and is interested in highest-impact-per-dollar additions. Has a documented set of original cocktails reflecting a clear creative voice.

equipment.glassware: Full coverage — coupe, rocks/Old Fashioned, highball, pint, Nick & Nora, martini

archetypes.name: Sophisticated
archetypes.description: Well-developed palate; rules out specific ingredients (Chartreuse, egg) based on taste rather than unfamiliarity

archetypes.name: Curious / Studious
archetypes.description: Wants to know why a drink works, engages with structural rationale, asks for 'ingredient logic' not just recipes

archetypes.name: Bartender's bartender
archetypes.description: Designs originals, maintains a documented archive, treats this as an ongoing creative discipline

archetypes.name: Spirit-forward / Old-school
archetypes.description: Manhattan-and-Old-Fashioned territory at the core, with modern willingness to experiment around the edges

archetype_notes: Glenn has the discipline of a classicist with the curiosity of a modernist. Not frou-frou, not theatrical, not minimalist. Treats cocktail-making the way he probably treats predictive modeling — structural, evidence-based, iterative, and willing to reject results that don't hold up.

disliked_ingredients.name: Chartreuse
disliked_ingredients.notes: Not Glenn's style. Excludes Last Word, Bijou, Naked & Famous, Final Ward, etc. Do not suggest as a purchase.

disliked_ingredients.name: Egg
disliked_ingredients.notes: No egg-based cocktails. Excludes flips, egg-white sours (Pisco Sour, Clover Club, Boston Sour, Whiskey Sour with foam, Ramos Gin Fizz, etc.), and any whole-egg or egg-white build. Eggs may be in the kitchen for cooking but are not used in drinks.

substitute_for_now.notes: Glenn enjoys tequila and plans to add it eventually. For citrus-forward shaken drinks (Margarita, Sidecar variants), the mezcal sub is excellent. For refresher-style cocktails (Paloma, Tommy's Margarita), the mezcal sub changes character meaningfully — flag the trade-off when relevant.

documented_originals_reference.name: Dave's Folly
documented_originals_reference.creator: Barkeeper Bjorn (AI)
documented_originals_reference.style: Shaken rye with pear and habanero

documented_originals_reference.name: The Crossover
documented_originals_reference.creator: Barkeeper Bjorn (AI)
documented_originals_reference.style: Shaken mezcal with citrus and Cocchi

documented_originals_reference.name: Smokey the Pear
documented_originals_reference.creator: Glenn Strycker
documented_originals_reference.style: Shaken mezcal + split base, pear-forward, desert-themed

profile_evolution_log.change: Initial profile created. All 6 flavor axes set with High confidence based on documented inventory, six originals, and conversation history. Drinker archetypes assigned: Sophisticated, Curious/Studious, Bartender's bartender, Spirit-forward/Old-school.
profile_evolution_log.reason: Migration from legacy single-file architecture to new repo structure (Barkeeper Bjorn v1.0).

guests.name: Dave
guests.preferences: Loves habanero-infused drinks. Tolerant of significant heat.
documented_drinks: cocktail5 — Dave's Folly (rye + crème de poire + habanero honey syrup)

guests.name: Stan
guests.preferences: Occasional drinker. No home bar yet. Profile not yet captured — Glenn is considering setting Stan up with his own Barkeeper Bjorn instance using the Minimalist track.

versioning.notes: Initial profile for Glenn Strycker. Migrated from legacy bartender.md 'Bar Owner' section. All 6 flavor axes calibrated High confidence based on documented patterns. Four drinker archetypes assigned. Two guests (Dave, Stan) noted.
